title: Discovery Service: Bitstring Status List Publication and Revocation Management
description: Implement the generation, update, signing, and publication of Bitstring Status Lists (W3C v1.0) for credential revocation and suspension. The module provides an admin revocation API, a public read-only status list endpoint for sentinels and verifiers, periodic auto-publish jobs, and integration hooks for on-chain anchoring (TASK-031). Every revocation is dual-person controlled in prod and fully audit-logged.
objectives: Operational revocation via admin API with latency under 60 seconds end-to-end
Status lists are signed, versioned, and cache-friendly (ETag, public CDN-compatible endpoint)
Audit trail for every revocation: who revoked, when, why, with what severity

steps: Step 1 - Status List Model: Each status list is owned by a (issuer_did, env, credential_type) triple. Status list ID: lowercase UUID or slug (e.g. prod-sentinel-identity-001). Bitstring: a bytes array of length = ceil(max_credentials / 8), stored in DB as BYTEA. Each credential gets a static index allocated at issuance time (call allocate_index() atomically). Index is immutable for credential lifetime.

Step 2 - StatusListService: Create services/discovery/src/discovery/services/status_list_service.py. Methods: allocate_index(issuer_did, env, credential_type) -> (status_list_id, index); set_bit(status_list_id, index, value: 0|1) -> None; get_status(status_list_id, index) -> 0|1; generate_status_list_credential(status_list_id) -> str (signed JWT); publish(status_list_id) -> None (persist + update hash).

Step 3 - Atomic Index Allocation: Use SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on the status_lists row to get the current top_index. Increment top_index. If top_index >= MAX_LIST_SIZE (default 131072): create a new status list for the same (issuer_did, env, credential_type) bucket and allocate from new list. Commit in single transaction. This prevents index collisions at high concurrency.

Step 4 - StatusListCredential JWT: Follows Bitstring Status List v1.0. JWT header: { alg: 'EdDSA', kid: '<key_id>', typ: 'statuslist+jwt' }. JWT payload: { iss, sub: status_list_url, iat, exp: iat + 24h, vc: { '@context': ['https://www.w3.org/ns/credentials/v2', 'https://www.w3.org/ns/credentials/status/v1'], type: ['VerifiableCredential', 'BitstringStatusListCredential'], credentialSubject: { id: status_list_url, type: 'BitstringStatusList', statusPurpose: 'revocation', encodedList: <base64url(gzip(bitstring_bytes))> } } }.

Step 5 - Public Status List Endpoint: GET /status/{status_list_id}. No authentication required — public endpoint for sentinels and any verifier. Returns the latest StatusListCredential JWT with Content-Type: application/jwt. Add: ETag (hash of encodedList), Cache-Control: public, max-age=60, must-revalidate. Support If-None-Match for 304 responses. This endpoint must be accessible without VPN in prod.

Step 6 - Revocation Admin Endpoint: POST /api/v1/credentials/{credential_id}/revoke. Auth: security-admin. Body: { reason: str (max 500 chars), severity: 'low'|'medium'|'critical', revoked_by: str }. Steps: (a) load credential record, verify status='active', (b) set bit at status_list_index to 1, (c) update credential status='revoked' in DB, (d) trigger publish(), (e) write audit event: action='credential_revoked', actor, credential_id, jti, reason, severity. Response 200: { revoked_at, status_list_id, status_list_index, next_publish_at }.

Step 7 - Suspension Endpoint (Optional): POST /api/v1/credentials/{credential_id}/suspend. Suspends a credential temporarily without permanent revocation. Uses a second status list with statusPurpose='suspension' if configured. Reversal: POST /api/v1/credentials/{credential_id}/unsuspend. Both audit logged.

Step 8 - Dual-Person Control in Prod: If env='prod' AND dual_control_enabled=true in settings: POST /revoke creates a pending_revocations record with status='PENDING_APPROVAL'. A second security-admin must POST /api/v1/credentials/{credential_id}/revoke/confirm. After confirmation, revocation proceeds. Rejection: DELETE /api/v1/credentials/{credential_id}/revoke/pending. Audit events for request, confirmation, and execution.

Step 9 - Periodic Publish Job: Background task (Arq or APScheduler) runs every 60 seconds. For each status list with dirty=true (modified since last publish): re-sign the StatusListCredential JWT, update current_hash, clear dirty flag, update published_at. Emit Prometheus metric: status_list_publish_duration_seconds, status_list_size_bytes.

Step 10 - On-Chain Anchoring Hook: After each publish, if ANCHOR_STATUS_LIST_ON_CHAIN=true: emit an internal event or call StatusRegistryClient.anchor_status_list(status_list_id, current_hash). TASK-031 implements the actual on-chain write. Store anchor_tx_hash and anchor_pending flag in status_lists table. This is best-effort — revocation does NOT wait for chain confirmation.

Step 11 - Rollover on Full Status List: When a status list reaches MAX_LIST_SIZE, it is frozen (no new indexes allocated). New credentials for the same bucket get a new status list ID. Old list remains published and read-only. Sentinels and verifiers must support following the statusListCredential URL to the current list.

Step 12 - Idempotent Revocation: Revoking an already-revoked credential returns 200 with { already_revoked: true, revoked_at: original_revocation_time }. Does not fail, does not re-write audit event. Allows safe retry from admin UI.

fields.name: bitstring
fields.comment: Raw bitstring, NOT gzip-encoded — encoding applied at JWT generation

fields.name: dirty
fields.comment: True if bitstring changed since last publish

error_handling.message: Revocation request created and awaiting second approval. Use /revoke/confirm to proceed.

error_handling.condition: status list not found for ID

security_constraints: Revocation endpoint protected by security-admin role (TASK-023)
In prod with dual_control_enabled: single admin cannot unilaterally revoke — second approval required
Every revocation writes an audit event with actor identity, credential jti, reason, and severity
Public /status endpoint does not require auth — but only returns the signed JWT, not bitstring raw data
set_bit operation is transactional — concurrent revocations on same status list are serialized via SELECT FOR UPDATE
StatusListCredential JWT signed with Discovery issuer key — sentinels must verify signature before trusting
On-chain anchoring is best-effort — revocation takes effect locally before chain confirmation

acceptance_criteria: POST /revoke sets the corresponding bit and publishes updated status list within 60 seconds
GET /status/{id} returns a valid, signed Bitstring StatusListCredential JWT
Bit for revoked credential is 1 in the published bitstring
Two concurrent revocations on different indexes succeed without corruption (concurrent test)
Revocation of already-revoked credential returns 200 with already_revoked=true (idempotent)
audit_events table contains a record for each revocation with actor, jti, reason, severity
ETag-based 304 response works for polling sentinels that have not seen a change
